ksa_text: Knowledge of semantic layer and metrics layer design, including measure definition, dimension hierarchy design, slowly changing dimension handling, metric definition standards, and centralized metric consistency across reporting tools.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Edge-domain Data Architecture & Analytics KSA with insufficient signal strength (composite score 0.4) to justify inclusion for the Chief of Staff to CDAIO. Without stronger framework alignment, this knowledge KSA does not meet the inclusion threshold.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Could this edge-domain knowledge KSA be expected in real-world Chief of Staff to CDAIO roles, even if not core to the role definition?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Job market analysis and framework role definitions confirm Data Architecture & Analytics is not a standard competency expectation for Chief of Staff to CDAIO. Edge-case exclusion is consistent with external evidence.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: external evidence does not support including this edge-domain KSA in the Chief of Staff to CDAIO competency profile. Exclusion maintained.

decision_rationale: Insufficient signal (score 0.4) and edge-domain placement (Data Architecture & Analytics) do not meet inclusion threshold for the Chief of Staff to CDAIO. All passes support exclusion.
